title: Today's Tech Brief: Litter-Robot Promo Codes and More
summary: A look at Litter-Robot deals, the Pope's AI warnings, and Anthropic's Vatican invite
imageAlt: Image from source article for Litter-Robot Promo Codes and Deals: Up to $150 Off
imageCaption: Litter-Robot Promo Codes and Deals: Up to $150 Off
imageSourceTitle: Litter-Robot Promo Codes and Deals: Up to $150 Off

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I've got a few interesting stories to share with you, from robotic litter boxes to AI warnings from the Vatican. Grab a cup of coffee, get comfortable, and let's dive in.

If you're a cat owner, you might want to check out the Litter-Robot, an automatic litter box that's highly rated by WIRED's Reviews team. I'm talking up to $150 off, which is a significant discount. The reviewer raves about it, saying it's the best investment they've made for their feline friend. No more scooping poop - that's a win in my book. I've heard it's a game-changer for cat owners, and I'm not surprised. Who wouldn't want to avoid the daily grind of litter box maintenance?

The Pope has weighed in on AI, and it seems like he might have used AI to write about it. Analyses suggest that parts of his recent statement, Magnifica Humanitas, were penned by a machine. It's a bit ironic, isn't it? The Pope warning us about AI, while possibly using it to craft his message. I'm not sure what's more surprising - that the Pope is thinking about AI, or that he might be using AI to think about it.

In a surprising move, the Vatican invited Anthropic's cofounder, Christopher Olah, to speak at the presentation of the Pope's AI encyclical. This unusual alliance between the Catholic Church and Silicon Valley has raised some eyebrows. What does this partnership mean for the future of AI? It's clear that the Vatican is taking AI seriously, and that's got me curious. What happens when technology and spirituality intersect?

These stories got me thinking about the intersections between technology, society, and our daily lives. From robotic litter boxes to AI warnings from the Vatican, it's clear that tech is having a profound impact on our world. Whether it's changing the way we care for our pets or the way we think about morality, technology is always evolving, and we're along for the ride.

Litter-Robot Promo Codes and Deals: Up to $150 Off is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: I always tell people that the best thing I’ve ever tested for my job here on the WIRED Reviews team is my automatic litter box from Litter-Robot. I recommend every cat owner invest in an automatic litter box. If they can swing it, the Litter-Robot is the best one to invest in. You’ll be able to say goodbye to the days of scooping and smells while this nifty... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: AI News Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI? Analyses have determined that parts of the Magnifica Humanitas appear to have been written by AI. Analyses have determined that parts of the Magnifica Humanitas appear to have been written by AI. by Jay Peters and Hayden Field May 27, 2026, 12:38 AM UTC Getty Images It’s possible that AI was used... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

As I look at these stories, I'm struck by the ways in which technology is seeping into every aspect of our lives. Whether it's a robotic litter box or AI-generated text, it's clear that we're living in a time of rapid change. And that's both exhilarating and unsettling. I'm Leo Stone, and that's all for today's tech brief. Until next time, stay curious, and keep on tech-ing.
